infidelities.paper_quote: Forest plots indicated that p217_MS and p217_Ratio provided the most substantial improvements in Area Under the Curve (AUC) relative to the p-tau181 benchmark (Figure 3A). ... (A) Forest plot showing the mean difference (MD) in AUC for detecting amyloid-β pathology relative to the p181_IA baseline, using a random-effects model
infidelities.note: All P-score numbers (0.859/0.783/0.686/0.117) exactly match Table 2 Outcome 1 and the running text (lines 199-208). However the added sentence 'In the forest plot vs. the p181_IA baseline, p217_MS had a mean AUC difference of MD = 0.10 (95% CI 0.04-0.16)' has no textual support anywhere in the paper: the fulltext (main text + Figure 3 caption) only describes the forest plot qualitatively ('most substantial improvements'), and no numeric MD or CI value for p217_MS vs p181_IA appears anywhere in the extracted text (searched '0.10', '0.04', '0.16' and bracket-CI patterns — no hits tied to this comparison). The only MD figure ever given anywhere in the paper is MD=0.025 for the ratio-vs-single-analyte gain (a different comparison, used correctly in C02).

infidelities.paper_quote: Forest plots confirmed that p217_MS and p217_Lumi provided significantly higher AUCs for Tau-PET recognition compared to p181_IA (Figure 3B). Notably, p-tau181 failed to reliably discriminate Tau-PET status (P-score = 0.090)
infidelities.note: P-scores (0.907 rank1 Tau-PET, 0.821 rank1 MCI-to-AD, 0.814 p217_Ratio, 0.090 p181_IA) all match Table 2 and §3.3/§3.4 text exactly. But the added clause 'In the Tau-PET forest plot, p217_MS had the largest AUC improvement over p181_IA (MD = 0.13, 95% CI 0.05-0.22)' is not supported anywhere in the fulltext — the paper only states the Figure 3B comparison qualitatively ('significantly higher AUCs'), with no numeric MD/CI value given in text for this comparison (searched '0.13', '0.05', '0.22' — no matching hits).

infidelities.paper_quote: Effect size: The primary effect size was the Mean Difference (MD) in AUC... Plasma p-tau181 measured by standard immunoassay (p181_IA) was set as the reference comparator.
infidelities.note: The four P-score numbers (0.117/0.159/0.090/0.006) and the '<0.20 across all outcomes' framing are all directly supported (Table 2 and §4.1 'our results (P-score < 0.20) suggest it is effectively obsolete'). However the added clause 'its AUC advantage over baseline was non-significant where measured' has no support: the word 'significant'/'non-significant' in this context never appears in the paper (grep for 'non-significant', 'not significant' returns zero hits), and the framing is incoherent given p181_IA was itself set as the reference/baseline comparator (lines 192-194), not a node being compared against a baseline for 'AUC advantage'. This looks like a fabricated statistical claim layered onto an otherwise faithful statement.
